---
title: "Welcome"
output: html_document
---

<style>

figure {
    display: block;
    margin-top: 0em;
    margin-bottom: 0em;
    margin-left: 20px;
    margin-right: 0px;
    float: right;
}

</style>

<!-- <div align = "justify"> -->

<figure><img src = "photo1.jpeg" width = "170px"></figure>I am a linguist working primarily on phonology and its interfaces. I am affiliated with the Second Language Acquisition Group at McGill University, and I am part of the project ‘Phonological effects on grammatical representation and processing’ (PIs: Heather Goad and Lydia White). My PhD is from Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul (UFRGS, Brazil).

My research programme investigates issues at the phonology-syntax interface, such as (i) the ways in which the application of phonological processes is constrained by prosodic structure, (ii) the acquisition of prosodic structures, and (iii) how bilingualism, language contact and language variation affect speakers' prosodic representations. My research focuses particularly on Romance languages.

Next Winter (2020), I'll be teaching LING350 -- Linguistic Aspects of Bilingualism and LING355 -- Language Acquisition at McGill.

You can email me at natalia.brambattiguzzo@mcgill.ca.



<!-- </div> -->
